pi_items.source: Task description explicitly specifies 3 sequential logs with dates May 19, May 26, and June 2, 2025
Task description states 'Date: May 19, 2025' for first log
Task description states 'Date: May 26, 2025' for second log
Task description states 'Date: June 2, 2025' for third log
Task description states 'Create three sequential Observation logs'

Task description Log 1 notes contain 'Corn at V3 stage (3 leaves with visible collars)'. Verified against USDA/University Extension sources: V3 means 3 leaves with visible collars, typically 2-4 weeks after emergence.

Task description Log 1 notes state 'Stand count averaged 32,000 plants per acre'. Verified against multiple university extension sources (Ohio State, Purdue, Minnesota): 32,000 plants/acre is the standard optimum plant population for corn grain production.
Task description states Log 1 has 'Quantity: 12 inches (Label: plant height)'

Task description Log 2 notes contain 'Corn at V6 stage. Growing point now above soil surface.' Verified against Pioneer Seeds and university sources: V6 stage corn is approximately 2 feet tall with growing point above soil surface and ear shoots/tassel initiation beginning.

Task description Log 2 notes state 'Minor flea beetle feeding on lower leaves, below economic threshold'. Verified against Purdue Extension and UC IPM: Flea beetles are a real corn pest; by V6 stage economic damage is unlikely as plants are past seedling vulnerability phase.
Task description states Log 2 has 'Quantity: 24 inches (Label: plant height)'. This is consistent with V6 stage description of 'about 2 feet tall' (24 inches).
Task description Log 3 notes contain 'Corn at V9 stage'. Verified against Pioneer Seeds and university extension sources: V9 stage is characterized by rapid stalk growth and tassel development.

Task description Log 3 notes state 'Tassel initiation beginning'. Verified: tassel initiation is a key characteristic of the V9 growth stage. At V6, ear shoots are initiated; at V9 the tassel is developing rapidly.
Task description states Log 3 has 'Quantity: 38 inches (Label: plant height)'. Consistent with V9 stage which involves rapid internode elongation and stalk growth.

pi_items.metadata_value: Applied side-dress nitrogen recommendation discussed in class
pi_items.verified_value: Side-dress nitrogen at V6 stage is a standard agronomic practice
pi_items.source: Task description Log 2 notes mention 'Applied side-dress nitrogen recommendation discussed in class'. Verified against multiple extension sources (Bayer Crop Science, Pioneer Seeds, University of Minnesota): V6 (V4-V8 range) is optimal timing for sidedressing nitrogen in corn production. By V6, corn has only taken up ~20-25 lbs N/acre and relies solely on soil-supplied nutrients.

privileged_info_summary: This task creates three Observation logs in farmOS Field Kit documenting corn growth monitoring across three weeks (May 19, May 26, June 2, 2025). All metadata values are verified: expected 3 logs with specified dates, log type 'observation', plant heights of 12/24/38 inches, and agricultural keywords including V3/V6/V9 growth stages, 32,000 plants/acre stand count, flea beetle damage, and tassel initiation. All agricultural information is accurate to real corn production practices. Data is synthetic (constructed educational scenario) with no external data sources.
